data.title: XRP's 2025 Price Surge Sparks Interest in PropiChain
data.description:
XRP's 2025 Price Forecast Sparks Interest in Rival Altcoin, PropiChain

Ripple's (XRP) price forecast has sent shockwaves across the cryptocurrency market, with experts believing that this projection could lead to a surge in other altcoins. Following XRP's price forecast, analysts have set their sights on PropiChain (PCHAIN), a new altcoin that simplifies investment and provides opportunities in the AI and real estate space.

PropiChain's recent market listing on CoinMarketCap signals its potential for a significant rally once it launches officially. The project leverages the real estate investment space, one of the oldest markets in the world, by eliminating the barriers of traditional property investment.

Crypto analyst Ali Martinez shared in a post on X that XRP was preparing for a possible breakout from its ascending triangle. The analyst shared that if XRP maintained its bullish outlook, the project could experience a 26% move, and many investors anticipate the results of this price forecast.

XRP is currently priced at about $2.7, surging over 11% within the past week due to positive investor sentiment surrounding the project. Recently, Brazil's Securities and Exchange Commission (CVM) approved a Hashdex XRP ETF, which shows its potential for mainstream adoption this year.

Experts predict that XRP will witness a surge of over 31% by May, and analysts say that this price forecast will trigger a rally for other altcoins. That's why top investors believe that the XRP price forecast could lead to PropiChain's massive rally.

PropiChain is a blockchain platform transforming how individuals invest in real estate. Unlike conventional investment systems, PropiChain offers a diverse range of features like tokenized real estate, a metaverse platform, and AI-driven data.

Although XRP's price forecast has sparked excitement among many investors, PropiChain emerges as the best altcoin for long-term investment. The tokenization of real estate is one of the key features that sets PropiChain ahead of other projects.

With PropiChain, you can access a variety of AI-driven tools to help you make informed choices. These tools are useful in analyzing real estate trends, rental income, and market fluctuations to ensure that you make the most of your investment.

data.title: FedAg Shares Plummet: Board Loss Shocks Market
MarketAxess CEO to Share Insights at Raymond James Conference
data.description:
MarketAxess Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: MKTX), the leading operator of an electronic trading platform for fixed-income securities, has announced that its CEO, Chris Concannon, will participate in the Raymond James 2025 Institutional Investors Conference on March 3, 2025. Mr. Concannon is scheduled for a fireside chat at 4:35 p.m. ET, where he will discuss the company's recent performance, strategic initiatives, and future prospects.

In his fireside chat, Mr. Concannon is expected to provide key insights into MarketAxess' strong financial performance in 2024, including record full-year revenue of $817 million, up 9% year-over-year (YoY). He will likely highlight the company's growth in emerging markets, Eurobonds, and municipal bonds, as well as its record rates commission revenue, up 21% YoY. Additionally, Mr. Concannon may discuss MarketAxess' record services revenue, up 18% YoY, and its expansion of client engagement with X-Pro.



Mr. Concannon is also expected to share updates on MarketAxess' strategic initiatives, such as the launch of its block trading solution in Eurobonds and emerging markets, which has seen early positive signs of success with a 22% increase in emerging markets block trading. He may also discuss the company's gain of over 200 basis points of U.S. high-grade market share and the launch of benchmark pricing for U.S. Credit and Eurobonds in the portfolio trading channel. Additionally, Mr. Concannon may provide insights into the company's plans to complete the integration of Dealer RFQ on X-Pro and the migration of Mid-X to Pragma technology in the dealer-initiated channel.



Investors can expect Mr. Concannon to emphasize MarketAxess' clear leadership position in the client-initiated channel and its ongoing expansion of client engagement with X-Pro. He may also discuss the company's strong geographic and product diversification, with a 14.8% increase in average daily volume (ADV) from international products, including emerging markets and Eurobonds. Additionally, Mr. Concannon may share his thoughts on MarketAxess' ongoing efforts to drive market share growth throughout 2025 and long-term value for shareholders by executing its strategy across the three market channels.

In conclusion, Chris Concannon's participation in the Raymond James 2025 Institutional Investors Conference is an excellent opportunity for investors to gain valuable insights into MarketAxess' recent performance, strategic initiatives, and future prospects. By attending the fireside chat, investors can better understand the company's growth strategies and make informed decisions about their investments in MarketAxess.

data.title: Gold Rush: Everest Shares Plummet After Hours
3 Key Takeaways from Warren Buffett's Annual Letter to Berkshire Hathaway Shareholders
data.description:
Warren Buffett, the legendary investor and CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, recently released his annual letter to shareholders. In it, he shared valuable insights, lessons, and reflections on the company's performance and his investment philosophy. Here are three key takeaways from the letter that investors and business leaders can learn from:

1. Transparency and honesty in business
Buffett emphasized the importance of transparency and honesty in business, particularly when it comes to admitting and addressing mistakes. He wrote, "The cardinal sin is delaying the correction of mistakes or what Charlie Munger called 'thumb-sucking.' Problems, he would tell me, cannot be wished away. They require action, however uncomfortable that may be." Buffett's candor about mistakes and his commitment to addressing them directly sets a strong example for other companies to follow.

2. Succession planning and leadership transitions
Buffett discussed his succession planning process, highlighting the importance of selecting a successor who aligns with the company's philosophy and culture. He named Greg Abel, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway Energy, as his successor in 2021 and has been gradually transitioning responsibilities to him. This approach emphasizes the importance of long-term planning, cultural fit, and a gradual transition process for successful leadership transitions.

data.title: Watch These Tesla Stock Price Levels as February Slump Accelerates
data.description:
As February rolls on, Tesla (TSLA) shareholders are grappling with a stock price that's been on a downward spiral, shedding 18% year-to-date. With the stock breaking down below key support levels, investors are left wondering where the bottom might be. Let's dive into the critical price levels to watch as the February slump continues.



Key Support Levels to Watch

1. $325 - The February Low
* Tesla shares briefly breached this level on Monday, and it could provide support near the February low.
* This area aligns with the lower range of a consolidation period situated just under the head and shoulders’ left shoulder.
2. $265 - The 200-day MA and Trendline Support
* If the bulls fail to defend the $325 level, the shares could fall to around $265.
* This region is in proximity to the closely watched 200-day MA and a trendline that connects multiple peaks on the chart extending back to September 2023.
* Additionally, this area sits just below a projected bars pattern downside target that takes the stock’s downtrend from mid-January to early February and overlays it from this month’s countertrend high. Such a move would complete an Elliot Wave pattern with five distinct swings before a corrective phase typically occurs.



Important Overhead Areas to Monitor

1. $430 - The Head and Shoulders' Right Shoulder Resistance
* During upswings in the stock, investors should initially keep track of the $430 area.
* The shares could face selling pressure in this region near the head and shoulders’ right shoulder.
2. $489 - The Record High and Neckline Resistance
* A close above the $430 level may propel a move to around $489.
* This chart location would likely attract significant attention near the stock’s record high, which also doubles as the peak of the head and shoulders pattern.

As the February slump continues, investors should keep a close eye on these key support and resistance levels. Breaking below these critical support levels could signal a further decline in the stock, while overcoming the overhead resistance areas could indicate a potential turnaround in Tesla's price action. By staying attuned to these price levels, investors can better position themselves to capitalize on potential opportunities in the stock.

In conclusion, as Tesla's stock price continues to decline in February, investors should watch for key support levels around $325 and $265, as well as important overhead areas near $430 and $489. By monitoring these critical price levels, investors can make informed decisions about when to buy or sell the stock, ultimately helping them navigate the volatile market landscape.

data.title: Agora (API.US) reported Q4 revenue of US$34.5 million, a 28.6% YoY increase, and a 28.6% YoY growth in net profit.
data.description:
Agora, Inc. (API.US), the parent company of Agora, Inc., has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. According to the report, Agora Q3 revenue was US$34.5 million, a 4.4% YoY decrease, in line with the company's previous expectation of being between US$34 million and US$36 million. Under the Non-GAAP accounting standards, net profit was US$1.8 million, a 28.6% YoY increase from US$1.4 million in the same period last year.

Business focusing on the Chinese market achieved revenue of RMB122.2 million (US$17.1 million), a 17.6% YoY decrease; Agora, focusing on non-Chinese markets, achieved revenue of US$17.4 million, a 13.7% YoY increase.

In terms of customer scale, Agora had 1,979 active customers as of December 31, 2024, a 7.8% YoY decrease, and Agora had 1,723 active customers, a 2.4% YoY increase.

For the full year of 2024, Agora's revenue was US$133.3 million, a 5.9% YoY decrease; under the Non-GAAP accounting standards, net loss was US$19.4 million, compared with a net loss of US$29.9 million in the same period last year. Business focusing on the Chinese market achieved revenue of RMB489.6 million (US$68.8 million), a 13.7% YoY decrease; Agora, focusing on non-Chinese markets, achieved revenue of US$64.5 million, a 5.7% YoY increase.

Looking ahead, based on the information currently available, the Company expects its revenue in the first quarter of 2025 to be between US$31 million and US$33 million, not including the revenue from certain terminal low-margin products.

data.title: "SEC Delays Solana ETFs, DTX Exchange Surges 800% in Presale"
data.description:
Regulatory uncertainty has led to a delay in the approval of spot Solana ETFs, with the SEC pushing back its decision to October 2025. This delay coincides with a 29% monthly decline in SOL's price. Meanwhile, the DTX Exchange presale has surged 800% to $0.18, positioning it as a high-growth opportunity for investors seeking alternative assets ahead of its Q2 launch.

The DTX Exchange presale has captured attention with an 800% price surge from its initial $0.02 offering, currently priced at $0.18. The project has raised $15 million from over 700,000 holders, making it one of the fastest-growing presales of 2025. This hybrid trading platform offers blockchain-powered access to 120,000+ assets, including stocks, forex, and ETFs, all within a single interface. Features like 1000x multipliers, copy trading, and the Phoenix Wallet have fueled demand, while its focus on privacy enables global traders to participate without identity verification. SolidProof audits further bolster credibility, addressing security concerns that often plague new projects.

Crypto analyst Jake Gagain summarized the sentiment on X: "DTX’s presale growth mirrors Solana’s 2023 rebound—except this time, the infrastructure is battle-tested. A top crypto to invest in before Q2." With tokens capped at 475 million and a $0.36 listing price imminent, early adopters anticipate substantial upside as the platform launches.

The SEC's decision to postpone approval for the Solana ETF has cast uncertainty over Solana's short-term trajectory. Analysts now estimate a 70% chance of approval by late 2025, with institutional interest remaining cautiously optimistic despite the regulatory setback. SOL's price has dipped 30% over the past month, reflecting broader market hesitation as traders await clarity on ETF timelines. Market activity surrounding Solana reveals a divided outlook, with the network's active addresses recently surpassing 1 million, indicating robust retail adoption, while the lack of ETF progress has tempered bullish momentum.

Industry figures emphasize that political shifts or revised regulatory frameworks might accelerate the Solana ETF timeline. For now, traders monitor derivatives markets,

data.title: Apollo Global: Expanding Real Estate Equity Platform with Bridge Investment Group Acquisition
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (NYSE: APO) has announced that its 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders will be held virtually on June 6, 2025, at 9:30 am ET. The record date for the meeting is April 14, 2025, and information on the virtual meeting will be included in the 2025 proxy statement. This meeting comes at an exciting time for Apollo, as the company continues to expand its real estate equity platform through strategic acquisitions.

Apollo, a high-growth, global alternative asset manager, seeks to provide excess return at every point along the risk-reward spectrum from investment grade credit to private equity. With approximately $751 billion of assets under management as of December 31, 2024, Apollo has a strong track record of delivering value to its clients and providing innovative capital solutions for businesses seeking growth.

One of Apollo's most recent strategic moves is the acquisition of Bridge Investment Group Holdings Inc. (NYSE: BRDG), a leading residential and industrial real estate investment firm. The all-stock transaction, valued at approximately $1.5 billion, is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals.

Bridge Investment Group, founded in 2009, has a proven track record of success in the real estate sector. Led by an experienced senior leadership team and over 300 dedicated investment professionals, Bridge's forward-integrated model, nationwide operating platform, and data-driven approach have fostered organic growth and consistently produced desirable outcomes across asset classes. The acquisition of Bridge is expected to be highly synergistic with Apollo's existing real estate equity strategies and leading real estate credit platform.



The acquisition of Bridge is expected to provide Apollo with immediate scale to its real estate equity platform and enhance its origination capabilities in both real estate equity and credit. Bridge manages approximately $50 billion of high-quality AUM in real estate products targeting both institutional and wealth clients, which is expected to be highly complementary to Apollo's existing real estate equity strategies and real estate credit platform. The transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to Apollo's fee-related earnings upon closing.

Apollo Partner and Co-Head of Equity David Sambur stated, "We are pleased to announce this transaction with Bridge, which is highly aligned with Apollo's strategic focus on expanding our origination base in areas of our business that are growing but not yet at scale. Led by a respected real estate team including Executive Chairman Bob Morse and CEO Jonathan Slager, Bridge brings a seasoned team with deep expertise and a strong track record in their sectors. Their business will complement and further augment our existing real estate capabilities, and we believe we can help scale Bridge's products by leveraging the breadth of our integrated platform. We look forward to working with Bob and the talented Bridge team as we seek to achieve the strategic objectives we laid out at our recent Investor Day."

Bridge Executive Chairman Bob Morse commented, "We are proud to be joining Apollo and its industry-leading team, who share our commitment to performance and excellence. This transaction will allow the Bridge and Apollo teams to grow on the strong foundation that Bridge has built since 2009 as we work to pursue meaningful value and impact for our investors and communities. With Apollo's global integrated platform, resources, innovation and established expertise, we are confident that Bridge will be positioned for the next phase of growth amid growing demand across the alternative investments space."

In conclusion, Apollo's 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders comes at an exciting time for the company, as it continues to expand its real estate equity platform through the acquisition of Bridge Investment Group. This strategic move is expected to provide Apollo with immediate scale, enhance its origination capabilities, and align with its strategic focus on expanding its origination base in growing areas of its business. As Apollo looks to the future, investors can expect the company to continue delivering value to its clients and providing innovative capital solutions for businesses seeking growth.

data.title: Westlake's Q4 2024 Earnings Call: Navigating Contradictions in Cost Strategies, PVC Trends, and Margin Guidance
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in Westlake's latest 2024Q4 earnings call, specifically including: Cost Reduction and SVI Strategy, PVC Demand and Pricing Trends, HIP Margin Guidance and Expectations, PEM Demand and Pricing Trends, HIP Volume Growth Expectations, Pricing Dynamics in the PVC Market, and PEM Performance and Pricing:



Strong Financial Performance in Housing and Infrastructure Products (HIP):
- Westlake's HIP segment achieved record income from operations of $807 million and an EBITDA margin of 24% in 2024, contributing 85% of overall segment operating income.
- This growth was driven by strong sales volume, particularly in Pipe & Fittings and side and trim products, which grew by 8% year-over-year.

Cost Reduction and Efficiency Measures:
- Westlake targeted $170 million in total cost reductions for 2024, surpassing expectations, with $50 million in cost reductions achieved in the fourth quarter alone.
- These efficiencies were achieved through logistical and procurement improvements, procurement optimization, and budget management.

Challenges in Performance and Essential Materials (PEM):
- The PEM segment's 2024 sales volume grew by 1% year-over-year, although EBITDA did not see the same level of improvement.
- The decline in PEM's profitability was due to lower sales prices, weaker global economic conditions, and extended maintenance outages.

Sustainability and Environmental Initiatives:
- Westlake was recognized by the Polymers for Europe Alliance for its efforts in reducing CO2 emissions, and it began a pilot program with a leading U.S. hospital chain to source post-consumer recycled PVC material.
- These initiatives are part of a broader strategy to enhance sustainability and reduce environmental impact.

Capital Deployment and Liquidity Management:
- Despite significant cash on hand, Westlake maintained a focus on disciplined capital deployment, returning approximately $325 million to shareholders in 2024.
- The company's strong balance sheet position, with a 15-year average debt maturity life, provides it with strategic flexibility to pursue growth opportunities.

data.title: DeepSeek fuels a fintech race, brokers hire AI engineers, and algorithm engineers are in high demand.
data.description:
e company news, the frenzy caused by DeepSeek has been continuously heating up the "Fintech competition" in the securities industry. So far, more than 30 securities companies have announced the completion of local deployment and updated the application scenarios. This fierce competition is actually a concentrated display of the Fintech strength of each securities company. In the process of improving Fintech strength, talent is undoubtedly one of the important foundations.

The Securities Times reporter noticed that the demand for algorithm engineers in securities companies has increased year by year. Ding Xiu, business director of Career International, a human resources service enterprise, said that AI (artificial intelligence) algorithm engineers are in short supply. From the recruitment cycle, it takes an average of 60 days from the release of the recruitment needs of securities companies to the entry of candidates for ordinary AI engineer positions.

However, although the talent gap continues to exist, salaries have not risen accordingly. In addition, the demand for algorithm engineers is mainly concentrated in large securities companies, which reflects that the current securities industry pays more attention to efficiency in Fintech investment and the Matthew effect is increasingly prominent.

data.title: Dogecoin Cloud Mining: A New Way to Earn Cryptocurrency
data.description:
Dogecoin, a cryptocurrency inspired by the popular "Doge" internet meme, has gained significant attention in recent years. Launched in 2013 as a "joke coin," Dogecoin quickly developed its own online community and reached a capitalization of $60 million in January 2014. Unlike other cryptocurrencies, Dogecoin's initial coin production schedule was fast, with 100 billion coins in circulation by mid-2015 and an annual increase of 5.256 billion coins since then.

Cloud mining, a process by which cloud mining service providers are rewarded with new Dogecoins by checking transactions on the Dogecoin blockchain, has emerged as a popular method for mining Dogecoin. This process involves many computers processing the same block of transactions simultaneously, with only one computer winning the reward of new coins. The node that solves a complex mathematical equation first wins and can append the newly confirmed block of transactions to the rest of the blockchain.

Dogecoin mining differs from other cryptocurrencies in several ways. These differences include the algorithms used for mining new currency, block time, difficulty, and reward. Dogecoin's mining algorithms, block time, and difficulty are all unique, contributing to its distinct mining process. Additionally, Dogecoin offers a fixed reward for each new block, unlike some other cryptocurrencies that have decreasing rewards over time.

To engage in Dogecoin cloud mining, users can choose a cryptokeying cloud mining service provider. These providers offer various contracts with different investment amounts and total net profits. For example, the "Experience Contract" requires an investment of $100 with a total net profit of $100 + $6.6, while the "WhatsMiner M30S+" contract requires an investment of $500 with a total net profit of $500 + $36.6. Other contracts, such as the "Bitcoin Miner S19k Pro" and "WhatsMiner M66S," offer higher investment amounts and corresponding net profits.

Cryptokeying cloud mining offers several advantages, including a $10 bonus for new registrations, reduced financial burden for purchasing and maintaining expensive equipment, a variety of cryptocurrencies for payment, an intuitive interface for beginners and experienced miners, and an affiliate program with referral rewards

data.title: Ethereum, Solana Slip Amid Crypto Market Rout; Grayscale Adds $65M BTC
data.description:
Ethereum and Solana, two of the world's leading cryptocurrencies, experienced temporary price drops on Tuesday, with Ethereum briefly falling below the $2,500 mark and Solana dipping below $140.

Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, had been trading around the $2,500 level for much of the day before dropping to an intraday low of $2,487.50. The price decline came amidst a broader sell-off in the cryptocurrency market, which saw the total market capitalization of all cryptocurrencies fall by more than $100 billion in a matter of hours.

Solana, a high-performance blockchain platform, also experienced a temporary price drop, with its native token SOL falling to an intraday low of $139.50. The price decline came as the broader cryptocurrency market continued to grapple with regulatory uncertainty and concerns about the potential impact of rising interest rates on riskier assets.

Despite the temporary price drops, both Ethereum and Solana have seen significant gains in recent months. Ethereum, in particular, has benefited from the growing popularity of decentralized finance (DeFi) applications and non-fungible tokens (NFTs), which have driven demand for the cryptocurrency.

Meanwhile, institutional investors continue to show interest in cryptocurrencies. On Tuesday, Grayscale, the world's largest digital asset manager, transferred 681.986 BTC to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $65.19 million. The transfer comes as Grayscale's Bitcoin Trust continues to see strong inflows, with investors pouring more than $1 billion into the fund in the first quarter of 2022 alone.

In other news, a whale, or large investor, deposited 3,000 ETH to Kraken, one of the world's largest cryptocurrency exchanges, three hours ago. The deposit, worth approximately $84.5 million, comes as Ethereum continues to gain traction among institutional investors and retail traders alike.

Similarly, Wintermute, a prominent market maker, withdrew over 106,000 SOL from Binance, the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange, in the past six hours. The withdrawal, worth approximately $16

data.title: TFI Soars 1.66% Post-US Redomicile Plans
BioCryst Pharmaceuticals' Q4 2024: Contradictions in ORLADEYO Patients, Medicare Reauthorization, and APeX-P Opportunities
data.description:
These are the key contradictions discussed in BioCryst Pharmaceuticals' latest 2024 Q4 earnings call, specifically including: ORLADEYO paid patient expectations, Medicare reauthorization predictions, and APeX-P regulatory and market opportunities.



ORLADEYO Revenue and Market Momentum:
- BioCryst reported revenue of $131.5 million for Q4, with $124.2 million from ORLADEYO, contributing to full-year revenue of $450.7 million with $437.7 million from ORLADEYO.
- ORLADEYO's revenue grew by 34% year-over-year, reflecting strong market momentum supported by Medicare coverage improvements due to the Inflation Reduction Act.

Clinical Program Advancements:
- BioCryst advanced BCX17725 for Netherton syndrome into the clinic and is progressing avoralstat for DME towards clinical trials.
- These advancements are aimed at serving under-met medical needs and aligning with the company's vision to bring life-changing treatments for patients.

Pediatric Clinical Trial Success:
- The APeX-P trial with the pediatric oral granular formulation of ORLADEYO demonstrated positive results with children achieving early and sustained reduction in monthly HAE attack rates.
- This success supports the submission of an NDA, which is on track for later this year, and highlights the need for pediatric oral prophylaxis options.

International Market Expansion:
- BioCryst saw increased ex U.S. revenue contribution, with 17.2 million or 13.9% of Q4 revenue and 51.7 million or 11.8% for the full year.
- The company expects continued growth in international markets, driven by increased confidence in ORLADEYO's efficacy and broader adoption of oral prophylaxis options.

data.title: Lloyds Shares Soar 1.33%: Annual Report Delights Investors
